So came across this eMarketer article. They appear to be mixing up Meli Dolar, $MELI's stable coin, with Mercado Coin, their volatile cryptocurrency 🤣 

Meli Dolar is designed to protect users against local currency devaluation and is fully backed by US dollars and liquid assets like Treasury bonds.

They are not pivoting away from stablecoins as the article implied. They are just moving on from the crypto currency they launched as a reward mechanism because it did not gain much traction. Meli Dolar will remain as its primary digital currency across Latin America for merchant payments, peer-to-peer transfers, and future rewards. I think it is an essential piece of its fintech operations.
